description: You are a Solar Energy Installation Manager evaluating dust accumulation impacts on a commercial PV system in the desert Southwest. Using PySAM's Pvwattsv8 module, model a 2000 kW DC PV system (Module type 0, Array type 0, Tilt 30°, Azimuth 180°, DC-to-AC ratio 1.2) to quantify the annual energy loss due to panel soiling. Compare a clean baseline (0% monthly soiling) against a soiled scenario with the following monthly percentage losses: [2, 3, 4, 5, 5, 5, 2, 2, 3, 4, 3, 2]. Use a weather file from the SAM installation's solar_resource directory (check /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt or search /opt/SAM for an Arizona .csv). Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/soiling_analysis.json with the following fields: system_capacity_kw (2000), tilt_deg (30), azimuth_deg (180), weather_file, annual_energy_clean_kwh, annual_energy_soiled_kwh, soiling_loss_kwh, soiling_loss_percent, monthly_soiling_profile ([2,3,4,5,5,5,2,2,3,4,3,2]), capacity_factor_clean_pct, and capacity_factor_soiled_pct. Calculate capacity factor using the formula: (annual_energy_kwh / (system_capacity_kw * 8760)) * 100.
